name: Turkish Chicken Kebab Recipe: Succulent Tavuk Şiş Explained
author: Elizabeth Taviloglu
description: Use an overnight marinade of yogurt, onion, garlic, and spices for succulent chicken kebabs made the Turkish way. With only 15 minutes of active prep, these are one of the best kebabs you'll make!

ingredients.ingredient: boneless, skinless chicken breasts or thighs
Garnish: dried oregano, sumac, and paprika (optional)

steps: Pat 1 1/2 pounds bonelss, skinless chicken breasts dry with paper towels.
Cut the meat into 1-inch pieces. Set aside.
Using the finest grater possible, grate 1 large onion and 2 garlic cloves over a medium bowl.
Pour the pulp and juice into a fine mesh strainer and, using a wooden spoon, press out the juice into a medium bowl. Discard the onion and garlic pulp; reserve the juice.

Stir together the onion-garlic juice , 1/2 cup plain yogurt , 3 tablespoons vegetable oil , 2 tablespoons tomato paste , 1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper , 1 teaspoon paprika , and 1 teaspoon kosher salt .
Add the cubed chicken and toss to coat.
Cover the bowl and refrigerate it for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight for maximum flavor.
Prepare a medium (350°F to 375°F) gas or charcoal grill. If using bamboo skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes to prevent them from catching fire on the grill.

Remove the chicken pieces from the marinade and thread them onto soaked bamboo or metal skewers. The chunks of chicken can touch, but avoid placing them too closely together to ensure even cooking. Discard any remaining marinade.
Sprinkle the kebabs generously with salt and place them on a preheated grill. Grill evenly on all sides, for about 12 minutes total.
Garnish with sumac, dried oregano, and paprika , if desired, and serve immediately.
